LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ABM JANITORIAL SERVICES - NORTHERN CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION GRANTED AS TO HARASSMENT AND FAILURE TO PREVENT HARASSMENT. NO SEVERE AND PERVASIVE CONDUCT THAT ALTERED THE CONDITIONS OF WORK. MOTION GRANTED FOR RETALIATION, NO ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION OR NEXUS WITH PROTECTED ACTIVITY. MOTION DENIED AS TO FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE. NO COPY OF FEHA CLAIM ATTACHED TO MOVING PAPERS. TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT RAISED: WHETHER PLAINTIFF CAN PERFORM THE ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS OF HER JOB WITHOUT ACCOMMODATION AND THE SEVERITY OF PLAINTIFF'S CONDITION; WHETHER DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN THE INTERACTIVE PROCESS IN GOOD FAITH; WHETHER DEFENDANT'S ACCOMMODATION OF ALLOWING DAYS OFF WAS A REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION. (RULINGS ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE FILED IN OPEN COURT, AND COPIES GIVEN TO COUNSEL AT HEARING.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER RE: TODAY'S RULING.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR 5797

MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. Plaintiff is ordered to appear for a medical examination at a time and place to be noticed by Defendant. The court finds that the untimely objections and failure to appear at the previously notice examination were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $450, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ABM JANITORIAL SERVICES - NORTHERN CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Matthew D. Marca, Esq. of Littler Mendelson P.C. (415) 399-8474 appearing for Defendant and moving party; John F. Henning III, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 255-7675 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Motion granted as to harassment and failure to prevent harassment. No severe and pervasive conduct that altered the conditions of work. Motion granted for retaliation, no adverse employment action or nexus with protected activity. Motion denied as to failure to accommodate. No copy of FEHA claim attached to moving papers. Triable issues of material fact raised: whether plaintiff can perform the essential functions of her job without accommodation and the severity of plaintiff's condition; whether defendant engaged in the interactive process in good faith; whether defendant's accommodation of allowing days off was a reasonable accommodation. (Rulings on Evidentiary Objections are filed in open Court, and copies given to counsel at hearing.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order re: today's ruling.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or, Alternatively, Order Compelling Pltf'S Deposition And Issuing Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
John Henning, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Margarita Velis

No other appearances, tentative ruling is adopted as modified. Defendant ABM Janitorial Services' Motion for terminating sanctions or, alternatively, order compelling plaintiff's deposition and issuing sanctions, granted in part, denied in part:
a. Motion for terminating sanctions denied;
b. Motion to compel plaintiff's deposition granted; plaintiff shall appear and provide testimony at a deposition to be held within fourteen days of notice of entry of this order, and at a date, place and time to be determined by defendant;
c. Motion for monetary sanctions granted: (i) sanctions shall issue in the sum of $500.00 against plaintiff's counsel for failure to comply with the court's previous order to compel, imposition of this sanction is stayed pending plaintiff's deposition; if plaintiff appear for her deposition, this sanction will be permanently stayed; (ii) plaintiff's counsel shall reimburse defendant for interpreters' and court reporters' fees for plaintiff failure to appear at deposition; and for the filing fee for this motion; and (iii) sanctions shall issue in the sum of $500.00 against plaintiff and her counsel for failure to cooperate with the discovery process to date. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
